mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which drives corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce a positive rank IC over 60-day forward returns in any 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase signals improving capacity utilization and manufacturing demand, triggering short-term positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 5-day forward returns during recovery phases, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase above 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of high-inflation trigger months over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal shows a negative or zero rank IC for 20-day returns across all tested expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect improved household balance sheets and construction sector activity, which propagate through supply chains to generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: HOUST growth fails to predict 60-day SPX direction with a statistically significant positive coefficient in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising inflation indicates stagflationary supply constraints, which erode corporate margins and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of stagflation trigger instances over a 3-year rolling window.
